title: Home
description: Mission statement and recent updates for Curio Archive.
text: Mission Statement Curio Archive is an independent historical encyclopedia documenting the on-chain history of Curio Cards (2017). Its purpose is preservation, not promotion. All entries are generated from structured blockchain data and updated regularly. The archive seeks to provide a permanent, neutral record of one of the earliest Ethereum-based NFT collections. Editorial Baseline Neutral language and factual framing. No speculative attribution of intent. Explicit unavailable-data notation when records are incomplete. Client-side search index added Added searchable index and lightweight in-browser filtering. Initial static generator scaffold Added markdown-driven generation for cards, artists, timeline, provenance, and archive pages.

title: Timeline
description: Chronological event record generated from the event dataset.
text: Timeline Method Events are sorted oldest to newest by available date fields from data/events.json. Collection launch documented Project documentation records the Curio Cards launch in May 2017. This date is used as the starting point in archive chronology. Card 17b misprint publication Project documentation lists the publication timestamp for the 17b duplicate deployment. The event is retained as a distinct entry in collection records. Institutional auction record published Christie's published lot records include a Curio Cards full-set sale context. Archive timelines use the published auction date as a dated institutional reference.

title: About / Methodology
description: Methodology and publication standards for Curio Archive.
text: About Curio Archive Curio Archive is an independent historical encyclopedia documenting the on-chain history of Curio Cards (2017). Its purpose is preservation, not promotion. Methodology Primary records are prioritized over secondary summaries. Claims are attributed to source category. Missing records are not inferred. Data Handling All entries are generated from structured blockchain data and updated regularly. Markdown content provides editorial narrative. JSON datasets provide structured record inputs. Generated pages preserve schema order and timestamped publication output. Record Intent The archive seeks to provide a permanent, neutral record of one of the earliest Ethereum-based NFT collections.
